verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is canonically a secreted, ECM-resident protein in its latent form. However, documented cell-surface anchoring occurs in specific cellular contexts: (1) in stimulated regulatory T cells and B cells via the transmembrane protein GARP, which stably binds latent TGF-β1 and enables surface-localized activation; (2) under overexpression conditions in multiple cell types via GRP78-mediated mechanisms. The surface LAP/TGF-β is wash-resistant when anchored to GARP, qualifying as a stable surface pool. This represents cell-state-induced (activation-dependent) and potentially tissue-restricted (immune-cell-dominant) surface display.
